status: revised DC rove plan after Fort McHenry and Greenbelt GeoJSON checks
utcDay: Sunday UTC day begins Saturday 2026-06-20 at 20:00 EDT; Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap is the primary Maryland opener because Fort McHenry gates close at 18:00. Count Greenbelt as two confirmed references: US-0668 and US-7973.

sources.label: CWops Solid Copy No. 193, February 2026, W8WZ “Activating DC”
sources.notes: Article describes the LBJ 7-fer, the 4-fer fallback at Columbia Island Marina/picnic tables, and the 15-park Cheetah route completed on 2025-12-22.

sources.label: GeoJSON boundary and trail checks
sources.notes: Fresh NPS ArcGIS/OSM GeoJSON files were cached for Greenbelt, National Capital Parks, Baltimore-Washington Parkway, and Star-Spangled Banner NHT. Sweetgum is inside Greenbelt, outside the NACA and BW Parkway polygons, and about 774 m / 2,540 ft from the official STSP centerline.

sources.label: Imported n-fer evidence from top activators
NPS Fort McHenry operating hours
sources.notes: Official NPS page updated 2026-05-20 says summer weekend grounds and parking lot hours end at 18:00, gates close then, and last weekend entry is 17:45.

sources.label: NPS LBJ Memorial Grove operating hours
sources.notes: Official NPS page says LBJ Memorial Grove is open year round from 06:00 to 22:00 and Columbia Island Marina is open 06:00 to midnight.

sources.label: Greenbelt Park Sweetgum and campground access
sources.notes: Sweetgum is first-come, year-round, and day-use; separate NPS material says the park is dawn-to-dusk and the campground has 24/7 camper access. At 20:00 EDT on 2026-06-20, sunset is about 20:36 EDT. Do not treat Sweetgum as the four-fer point; use a legal public point inside the Greenbelt/BW Parkway overlap.

sources.label: Local activator email feedback, June 2026
sources.notes: KB3WAV corroborated Fort McHenry gate closure; W3LAM corroborated LBJ vehicle access and seven-reference right-side parking-area operation; N2NWK suggested Greenbelt as a four-reference MD/DC-area option.

countingNotes: Count unique POTA entities on the Sunday UTC day, not local calendar day.

Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway is the primary Maryland opener for the Sunday UTC rove. Count US-0668 + US-7973 only from a legal public point in the overlap; US-0654 and US-4581 remain unconfirmed at Greenbelt unless an exact activator coordinate verifies under POTA rules.
Fort McHenry is not usable at/after 20:00 EDT: official NPS summer weekend gate closure is 18:00. Treat it as a pre-18:00 optional Saturday-local stop only.

POTA multi-loc activations require the activator and all station equipment to be inside the overlapping valid area for every declared reference; trail references require public land within 30.5 m / 100 ft of the trail.

The LBJ 7-fer is corroborated by W8WZ article evidence and local W3LAM feedback: the right side of the parking area / LBJ operating area is inside US-0784, US-0670, US-4564, US-4567, US-4581, US-9901, and US-9898.
Meadowood BLM SRMA is a single-reference Virginia fallback if Mason Neck hours, restrictions, or Sunday pace make the 2-fer impractical.

timelineAssumptions.notes: Timeline is a planning model, not a schedule commitment. Use 45 minutes per activation stop for planning, with 30 minutes as the absolute floor if the day needs to compress.

primaryTimeline.movementFromPrior: Saturday route arrival from Fox Point; use a legal public point in the Greenbelt/BW Parkway overlap, only while access is lawful before dusk
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Primary Maryland opener after Fort McHenry access check. GeoJSON supports a Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway 2-fer (US-0668 + US-7973); US-0654 and US-4581 remain unconfirmed at Greenbelt.
Sunday morning jackpot after Greenbelt. Adds seven unique references after the Greenbelt 2-fer; local feedback says this can be vehicle/base-station friendly.
Adds FDR and National Mall if National Mall has not already been counted. Walking from LBJ is about 29 minutes; rideshare is about 7 minutes.
Short Mall walk; National Mall is already duplicate after FDR.
Adds Lincoln; still before the Cheetah threshold in the Greenbelt 2-fer route.
Cheetah threshold with the Greenbelt 2-fer and LBJ 7-fer.
End of the W8WZ article-backed Mall sequence.

primaryTimeline.label: Return to Foggy Bottom hotel / get car
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Walk back to the hotel to pick up the car. Rideshare is about 9 minutes if schedule or weather makes the walk unattractive.
Ostrich finish if the Mason Neck 2-fer verifies and park/refuge access and antenna restrictions check out. This assumes the car stays at the hotel during the DC rove.

primaryTimeline.label: Fredericksburg hotel
primaryTimeline.timelineNote: Booked Sunday night anchor after the rove.

optionalTimelineAddOns.placement: Use instead of Mason Neck if the day is running late or refuge/park restrictions make the 2-fer unattractive.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineImpact: Adds about 1h16 before the Fredericksburg drive; about 18 minutes less driving than the Mason Neck option but one fewer entity.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineNote: Meadowood is the simpler VA backup, not an Ostrich-equivalent replacement for Mason Neck.

optionalTimelineAddOns.placement: between Washington Monument or Lincoln and Mason Neck
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineImpact: Adds about 1h 35m before Mason Neck when including the activation and extra driving.
optionalTimelineAddOns.timelineNote: Useful Ostrich buffer if public access and pace are excellent.

operatingStops.timing: Saturday 2026-06-20, activate at/after 20:00 EDT only from a legal public point in the Greenbelt/BW Parkway overlap and before access closes
operatingStops.name: Greenbelt Park / Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap
operatingStops.evidence: N2NWK email suggested a Greenbelt four-fer (US-0668 + US-7973 + US-0654 + US-4581). Fresh GeoJSON confirms a practical Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap for US-0668 + US-7973; strict four-way overlap is empty, and the best US-0668/US-0654/US-7973 component misses the official Star-Spangled Banner NHT 100-foot trail buffer by about 35 feet.
operatingStops.access: Use only a legal public operating point in the confirmed overlap and only while park access is lawful before dusk. Sweetgum is not the decision point for the overlap; campground access is not needed unless the only legal access after dusk is through a reserved campsite.
operatingStops.confidence: high-confidence two-reference Maryland opener; US-0654 and US-4581 remain unconfirmed at Greenbelt

operatingStops.timing: Optional Saturday-local stop only if arrival is early enough for a real activation before the 18:00 EDT gate closure; not available for Sunday UTC rollover
operatingStops.name: Fort McHenry National Monument
operatingStops.evidence: Repeated imported top-activator records show US-0928 + US-4581 + US-4567 + US-4582, but official NPS access hours rule out an at/after-20:00 Sunday UTC activation.
operatingStops.confidence: strong n-fer evidence before closing; skip if Fox Point cannot be left by about 16:00 for a useful activation window

operatingStops.timing: Sunday morning; primary jackpot after the Greenbelt MD opener
operatingStops.name: LBJ Memorial Grove exact 7-fer position
operatingStops.evidence: CWops/W8WZ article gives reference list; W3LAM email corroborates the right-side parking-area/LBJ operation and says vehicle, wires, stakes, tripods, and larger portable setups have worked there.
operatingStops.confidence: high after local activator feedback; bring headphones for DCA/Pentagon-area noise

operatingStops.name: Vietnam Veterans Memorial
operatingStops.confidence: article-backed; buffer beyond Cheetah after Greenbelt and LBJ

openVerification: Confirm the exact LBJ 7-fer/right-side parking-area operating position in the field, but local W3LAM feedback significantly raises confidence.
Do not rely on Fort McHenry for Sunday UTC: official NPS summer weekend gate closure is 18:00. Treat Fort only as optional Saturday-local if arriving before last entry.
Confirm Mason Neck NWR operating restrictions: POTA cache says free-standing antennas only, no vegetation attachment, no off-trail operating, and no tables in parking areas.
Check closing/dusk times for Mason Neck if using it as the Ostrich add-on.
Decide whether the friend meetup happens inside the rove sequence or after the Cheetah count is already secure.
Pennsylvania Avenue NHS and C&O Canal did not return clean OSM GeoJSON in the first fetch pass; verify boundaries before relying on those as easy add-ons.
Jefferson and Ford resolved as point GeoJSON rather than full polygons; use POTA/NPS context and on-site signage before treating the point as the whole activatable area.
Keep Meadowood BLM SRMA as the simple VA backup if Mason Neck access, hours, or pace do not work.

Confirm legal public access/parking for the Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap at or near 38.9927921,-76.8880143; count only US-0668 + US-7973 unless exact local confirmation supports more.
Ask N2NWK or another local activator for exact Greenbelt coordinates before relying on US-0654 or US-4581 there; strict four-way GeoJSON overlap is empty.
Fort McHenry decision rule: only go if Fox Point is finished early enough to reach Fort by about 17:00-17:15; otherwise skip it and protect the 20:00 Greenbelt opener.

expandedMallInventory.summary: DC cache has 32 references total. For the revised Foggy Bottom / National Mall rove, the practical inventory is the Greenbelt/BW Parkway 2-fer opener, the LBJ trail cluster, and 17 Mall/downtown references; Fort McHenry is optional before 18:00 only.

expandedMallRoute.name: Greenbelt 2-fer + LBJ + expanded Mall route
expandedMallRoute.strategy: Use the Greenbelt/BW Parkway overlap as the Saturday-night Maryland opener, LBJ as the Sunday jackpot, then walk the W8WZ Mall sequence. Greenbelt plus LBJ reaches 9 unique references before the Mall; Cheetah is reached at Vietnam and the full Mall route reaches 18 before the Virginia finish.

recommendedOrder: Greenbelt/Baltimore-Washington Parkway overlap after 20:00 EDT Saturday
Optional early: Theodore Roosevelt Island by car/rideshare if chasing 20+
Thomas Jefferson Memorial or FDR Memorial rideshare drop
Return to Foggy Bottom hotel, then drive to Mason Neck

countMilestones.label: After FDR starts the Mall overlay

expandedMallRoute.caution: Greenbelt is a confirmed 2-fer only for US-0668 + US-7973 from a legal overlap point; US-0654 and US-4581 need exact confirmation. Fort McHenry is no longer viable after 18:00.

routeOptimization.summary: Revised after Fort McHenry access and Greenbelt GeoJSON checks: Greenbelt/BW Parkway is the primary Maryland opener as a confirmed 2-fer, LBJ is the high-confidence Sunday jackpot, Cheetah is reached at Vietnam, and Mason Neck reaches Ostrich exactly if it verifies.
constraints: The Tesla stays parked at the Foggy Bottom hotel during the DC rove.
Prefer walking when the time penalty is modest and it avoids another rideshare.
Use rideshare for the hotel -> LBJ and LBJ -> Mall jumps; those crossings are not worth walking during a count-focused rove.
Return to the hotel before Mason Neck, because the southbound car move starts there.

recommendedCoreOrder.movement: 4 min from hotel; 9 min onward to LBJ
recommendedCoreOrder.value: +1 entity, but costs an extra early stop/ride

recommendedCoreOrder.value: +7 unique after Greenbelt, 7 refs total at the spot

recommendedCoreOrder.label: Mason Neck

addOnFindings.label: Theodore Roosevelt Island
addOnFindings.finding: Only do it before LBJ if chasing 20+. Walking hotel -> TR -> LBJ is about 88 minutes; rideshare movement is about 13 minutes before activation time.

addOnFindings.label: LBJ to FDR
addOnFindings.finding: Treat this as a rideshare jump. Walking is about 29 minutes; rideshare/driving directions are about 7 minutes.

addOnFindings.label: Return to hotel
addOnFindings.finding: After the Washington Monument core finish, walking back to Foggy Bottom is about 31 minutes; rideshare is about 9 minutes. Either way, get the car at the hotel before going south.

addOnFindings.label: East Mall expansion
addOnFindings.finding: If you push to Eisenhower, rideshare back to the hotel is about 11 minutes; walking back is about 54 minutes and should not be the default.

addOnFindings.label: Mason Neck
addOnFindings.finding: Preferred clean southbound 2-fer finish. From Washington Monument, current route check is about 41 minutes to Mason Neck and 53 minutes onward to Fredericksburg.

addOnFindings.label: Meadowood
addOnFindings.finding: Use as the simpler Virginia backup if Mason Neck is too late or too constrained. Washington Monument -> Meadowood -> Fredericksburg is about 1h16 driving, roughly 18 minutes less than the Mason Neck line, but it only adds US-9937.

addOnFindings.label: Fort McHenry Saturday branch
addOnFindings.finding: Fox Point -> Fort is about 1h12 and Fort -> Greenbelt about 43m. Only attempt Fort if Fox Point is finished by about 16:00 and Fort arrival is around 17:00-17:15 or earlier; otherwise skip it and protect the 20:00 Greenbelt opener.
